ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker, via ZOOM, counsel for Debra Dolch, Trustee; John Smalley, via ZOOM, Beneficiary, in propria persona; Stephen Smalley, via ZOOM, Beneficiary, in propria persona.

Court begins by querying counsel regarding why there would be no bond required if this matter is removed from court supervision. The court reminds counsel that probate code requires a bond if a person who is not named in the Trust is appointed Trustee. Additionally, if a trust is removed from court supervision, the bond increases. Counsel reports that there are three real properties which the family wishes to sell and that it would take less time to do if the Trust was not under court supervision. Counsel presents additional oral argument to support his petition. The court then turns to John Smalley regarding whether he has anything to add, he does not. The court then turns to Stephen Smalley who advises that he would prefer that a bond be required in light of the pending sale of the properties with their combined current fair market value of more than six million dollars.

After additional discussion, including the confirmation of the current bond amount of $2,154,000, the Probate Accounting Petition is taken under submission with the intent to grant. The court also advises the parties that it may require an additional bond in an amount to be determined by the court.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Heard with case #: PCN-10-294149. THE CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOHN PATRICK SMALLEY.

Appearances: Elizabeth Hasen for and with Stephen Smalley. Betty Epstein for and with Deborah Dolch. Ernest Der (Court Appointed Attorney) for John Patrick Smalley.

The Court reminded Ms. Hansen that papers should be filed at least three days prior to the hearing and tardiness hinders the Court from considering the documents. Ms. Hansen stated that there are details lacking in the accounting submitted, proposed that the survivor's trust and decedent's trust be divided, questioned the actual duties preformed by the Trustee, and inquired why pay roll expenses have not decreased. Ms. Epstein held discussion in response to issues raised (response filed by Ms. Hansen is not verified, Mr. Mur is not living at the residence, the Visa Credit Card, the property management company, construction on the property, prior renovation, Mr. Smalley living at Alma Via, and the car.)Furthermore, Ms. Epstein addressed the request for 1% fees and stated that this is a time consuming case. Mr. Der stated he believes that Mr. Smalley is in the best place possible to receive the care that he needs. Ms. Hansen will file a verification. Court takes a recess at 10:45a.m.

At 11:40 a.m., the matter is recalled. Ms. Epstein reported that Ms. Smalley and Ms. Dolch have offered to hold a monthly meeting at the house for the children to address any concerns and review a copy of the her visa credit card statments. The Court requested that for future accounting Ms. Dolch on the caregiver and paychecks section, provide a breakdown as to which caregiver are for Mr. Smalley and which are for Ms. Smalley, and a breakdown on what Mr. Mur is being paid. The Court also noted that the retirement checks are going into the trust and a waiver of future accounting in the Conservatorship is appropriate. A one year accounting for the dates October 1, 2012 to September 30, 2013 is required for the trust matter. Mr. Der is to stay on thru the second accounting. The matters are taken under submission.

Off Calendar Apr-18-2011 Continued To Jun-06-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Helene Wenzel for and with Thomas Lucas (Conservator and Proposed Successor Trustee). Ernest Der Court appointed for John Smalley. Grant Tomioka for and with Beatrice Smalley.

Ms. Wenzel presented oral argument to support her position with regard to Mr. Lucas being appointed as permanent Trustee, stated that all of the real property is in the Trust, that the Letters in the Conservatorship have been recorded as well as the order appointing Mr. Lucas as Temporary Trustee, that a Forensic Accountant will do a preliminary accounting by the end of the week and she expressed her concerns. Mr. Tomioka stated that Ms. Smalley opposes Mr. Lucas being appointment as permanent Trustee, and that she has no opposition to bringing the Trust under Court supervision and continuing to have Mr. Lucas as Temporary Trustee for time to communicate with Ms. Dolch to see if she will consider filing her petition. In addition, Mr. Tomioka pointed out that Ms. Samlley's capacity is still at issue. Mr. Der agrees with Mr. Tomioka's position. The matter was continued to June 6, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Wenzel will prepare an order extending appointment of the Temporary Trustee to June 6, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition For Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Hansen for and with Patrick Smalley, Christine Smalley So, and also for Stephen Smalley (not present). Court-Appointed Counsel Grant Tomioka for and with Beatrice Smalley. Helene Wenzel for and with Tom Lucas (Temporary Conservator) for Beatrice and John Patrick Smalley. Brian Kulich for and with T. Anne Lassahn (Private Professional).

The Court stated that there were two competing petitions to bring the Trust under Court supervision and that the second petition requests Ms. Lassahn serve. The Court also stated that it had received an envelope from Mrs. Smalley which it was returning in open court for the reasons set forth on the record. Mr. Tomioka presented argument on behalf of his client in support of the appointment of Ms. Lassahn. Helen Wenzel presented argument in support of her client. Mr. Kulich stated that there is no relationship with Patrick Dana, that Mrs. Smalley nominated his client, stated that he mis-stated some information, requested that the Court strike the Nelo Pratto reference, and that Mrs. Smalley can pick someone else to act. Mrs. Smalley presented her concerns for the record. Ms. Hansen stated that Ms. Lassahn's petition wasn't properly served, and presented argument in support of the appointment of Tom Lucas. Mr. Der stated that his client can't speak but that he supports the appointment of an independent. Ms. Wenzel presented concerns regarding litigation counsel and the attached agreement. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court granted the petition appointing Tom Lucas as Temporary Successor Trustee. The Court continued the Successor Trustee petitions to April 18, 2011 at 11:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR #11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
